LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CITY OF MODESTO MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED TO CORRECT ERRORS DESIGNATED #2 IN NOTICE OF MOTION. MOTION DENIED AS TO #1, TO ADD ALLEGATIONS OF MALICIOUS, FRAUDULENT AND OPPRESSIVE CONDUCT CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DELAY NOT JUSTIFIED IN VIEW OF FACTS KNOWN AT TIME OF FILING OF THE COMPLAINT, LACK OF DILIGENCE IN DISCOVERY, PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES, AND PLAINTIFF DILATORILY HIRING EXPERT. PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT WHO MUST PREPARE FOR JANUARY TRIAL AND COMPLETE DISCOVERY. THE COURT ADDS: RULING IS MADE WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THESE ISSUES BEING RE-RAISED BEFORE THE TRIAL JUDGE. ALSO, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT'S RULING. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint, Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Dec-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CITY OF MODESTO Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Appearances: Moris Davidovitz, Esq. Davidovitz & Bennett LLP (415) 956-4800 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Sharon R. Lewis, Esq. of Lewis, Brisbois, Bisgaard & Smith LLP appearing for Defendants ISOP and opposing party. Rebecca R. Weinreich, Esq. of Lewis, Brisbois, Bisgaard & Smith LLP Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants ISOP and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted to correct errors designated #2 in notice of motion. Motion denied as to #1, to add allegations of malicious, fraudulent and oppressive conduct claim for punitive damages. Delay not justified in view of facts known at time of filing of the complaint, lack of diligence in discovery, plaintiff's responses to discovery requests regarding punitive damages, and plaintiff dilatorily hiring expert. Prejudice to defendant who must prepare for January trial and complete discovery. The Court adds: ruling is made without prejudice to these issues being re-raised before the Trial Judge. Also, Plaintiff's counsel will file an Amended Complaint in compliance with the Court's ruling. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE STATE OF PENNSYLVANIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Moris Davidovitz, Esq. of Davidovitz & Bennett LLP (415) 956-4800, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; James English, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 749-3660 appearing for Plaintiff City of Modesto; Rebecca R. Weinreich, Esq. of Lewis, Brisbois, Bisgaard & Smith LLP, appearing for Defendant ICSOP and moving party; David J. Garthe, Esq. of Boornazian Jensen & Garthe (510) 834-4350, appearing for Defendant ACCEL; Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Denied, without prejudice. (If the trial date is continued motion may be re-noticed if there is an appropriate time frame for bringing the motion.) Motion fails to comply with CRC Rule 3.1350, subsections (b) and (g). Evidence introduced in support of motion also fails to comply with CRC Rules 3.1110, 3.1113 and 3.1116. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.)
